summary: Small matching-focused fix in Link hookshot item code. The PR adjusted matrix API usage and local-variable/control-flow shape in src/melee/it/items/itlinkhookshot.c, specifically around it_802A6F80 and it_802A7168, to improve or restore matches without changing the apparent high-level behavior.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; contains Link hookshot item matching fixes in it_802A6F80 and it_802A7168.

what_changed: In it_802A6F80, PSMTXIdentity calls were changed from explicit casts like PSMTXIdentity((MtxPtr) &m1) and PSMTXIdentity((MtxPtr) &m0) to direct array arguments PSMTXIdentity(m1) and PSMTXIdentity(m0).

In it_802A6F80, matrix concatenation order/output was changed from PSMTXConcat((MtxPtr) &m0, (MtxPtr) &m1, (MtxPtr) &m0) followed by HSD_JObjCopyMtx(arg0, m0) to PSMTXConcat(m1, m0, m1) followed by HSD_JObjCopyMtx(arg0, m1).
In it_802A7168, unused or match-hostile locals were removed, including vec_5, item_link_prev, item_link_prev_2, and a manual f32 pad[2], with PAD_STACK(4) used instead.
In it_802A7168, fighter access was normalized from arg0->owner->user_data to GET_FIGHTER(arg0->owner).
In it_802A7168, the Link kind comparison was normalized from the literal value 6 to FTKIND_LINK.

In it_802A7168, list-neighbor access was reshaped: the first neighbor position selection now checks item_link->next instead of storing item_link->prev in a temporary, while the later previous-position selection directly checks item_link->prev.
In it_802A7168, JObj access was normalized from item_link->x1D0_GObj->hsd_obj to GET_JOBJ(item_link->x1D0_GObj).

In it_802A7168, temporary vector roles were renamed/reassigned: item_link->pos is stored in vec_3 for translation and passed as the origin to it_802A6F80, while vec_4 now holds the delta vector passed to it_802A6F80.
smart_moves: Used project macros GET_FIGHTER, GET_JOBJ, FTKIND_LINK, and PAD_STACK instead of raw field access, magic constants, and hand-written padding, making the source more idiomatic while still serving matching.
Changed the matrix concat destination/source arrangement so the copied matrix is m1, matching the apparent required generated code shape for this function.
Removed temporary ItemLink pointer aliases and accessed item_link->prev or item_link->next directly, which likely better matched the original compiler's register/local allocation pattern.
Kept the change tightly scoped to one item file and two functions, reducing risk while fixing matching issues.
decomp_lessons: For Mtx/MtxPtr APIs, direct array arguments such as PSMTXIdentity(m1) can be preferable to explicit casts like (MtxPtr) &m1 when seeking a match.

Matrix operation order and destination choice can matter for matching even when the computed transform looks similar at a high level; preserve the original source/destination pattern when evidence points to it.
Prefer established project macros for common object access and constants, such as GET_FIGHTER, GET_JOBJ, and FTKIND_LINK, because they can improve both readability and matching consistency.
Use PAD_STACK when the diff indicates padding is needed for stack shape rather than keeping artificial named padding arrays.
Temporary variable pressure and vector local assignment order can be critical in matching item transform code; reusing or renaming Vec3 locals may affect generated code.

Neighbor traversal in linked item structures should be expressed in the same direction and access pattern as the target assembly; changing prev/next checks can be a matching tactic rather than a semantic refactor.

naming_conventions.convention: Use FTKIND_LINK instead of numeric fighter kind literals.
Use GET_FIGHTER(gobj) for fighter data access.
Use PAD_STACK(n) for intentional stack padding.
naming_conventions.evidence: f32 pad[2] was replaced with PAD_STACK(4).

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Remove explicit MtxPtr casts around local matrix arrays when direct array decay gives the desired call form.
Change PSMTXConcat argument order and output buffer to match target codegen: this PR used PSMTXConcat(m1, m0, m1) and copied m1 into the JObj.
Replace manual padding locals with PAD_STACK to control stack layout without introducing misleading semantic variables.
Avoid extra pointer temporaries when direct field access produces better register allocation or instruction ordering.
Reshape Vec3 locals so the translated position and delta vector occupy the locals expected by the target function.
Use project object-access macros because macro expansion can match canonical decomp patterns better than open-coded field access.

review_feedback.detail: No review comments were present in the provided PR context, and the human text excerpt was empty.

follow_up_queries: Find other PRs touching src/melee/it/items/itlinkhookshot.c for hookshot matching patterns.
Search for PSMTXConcat calls where source and destination matrix order affected matching.
Search for PAD_STACK replacements of manual f32 pad arrays in item code.
Search for conversions from raw user_data or hsd_obj field access to GET_FIGHTER and GET_JOBJ macros.
Investigate whether item_link->prev versus item_link->next access patterns recur in other ItemLink matching fixes.
